articles.title: GPT-4o Outperforms Junior Associates in Contract Review Study
articles.summary: A new benchmark study from Stanford Law finds that GPT-4o correctly identifies material adverse change clauses in 91% of test contracts, compared to 78% for first-year associates, raising fresh questions about training and deployment in law firms.

articles.title: SRA Publishes Binding AI Use Guidance for Solicitors
articles.summary: The Solicitors Regulation Authority has released its first binding guidance on the use of generative AI tools, requiring firms to maintain human oversight of AI-generated advice and disclose AI use to clients in writing.

articles.title: In-House Teams Embrace Agentic AI for Routine Legal Work
articles.summary: A survey of 400 general counsel and CLOs finds that 62% have piloted agentic AI workflows for NDAs and compliance reviews, with most reporting 30-40% time savings. Chief legal officers cite vendor due diligence as the next frontier.

articles.title: Harvard CLP: Law Schools Must Overhaul Curriculum for the LLM Era
articles.summary: A new Harvard Center on the Legal Profession report argues that traditional legal education inadequately prepares students to work alongside machine learning tools. It calls for mandatory AI literacy modules and practical skills training across all three years of law school.

articles.title: BigLaw's Hourly Billing Model Faces Existential Pressure from AI Efficiency
articles.summary: Legal Evolution's latest analysis shows that AI-driven efficiency gains are compressing associate hours on discovery and due diligence by up to 50%, forcing law firms to reconsider value-based and subscription pricing models to maintain revenue.

articles.title: CCBE Calls for Lawyer-Specific Exemptions in EU AI Act Implementation
articles.summary: The Council of Bars and Law Societies of Europe has submitted a position paper urging the European Commission to clarify how the AI Act applies to legal professional privilege, arguing that current compliance obligations conflict with bar association ethics rules.

articles.title: Courts Begin Requiring Disclosure of AI-Assisted E-Discovery
articles.summary: Several federal district courts have issued standing orders requiring parties to disclose when AI tools are used in e-discovery document review, signalling a broader shift toward AI transparency obligations in litigation.

articles.title: UK Legal Aid Agency Pilots AI Chatbot for Self-Represented Litigants
articles.summary: The Legal Aid Agency has launched a pilot chatbot to help self-represented individuals navigate civil claims, addressing the unmet legal need gap. Early data shows a 40% reduction in form errors among pilot users.

articles.title: Legal Operations Teams Drive Contract Automation at Scale
articles.summary: A roundtable of senior legal ops professionals highlights how automation platforms are eliminating manual contract routing, with some corporate legal departments processing over 10,000 routine agreements per year without attorney review.

articles.title: GDPR Enforcement Action Targets Law Firm AI Training Data Practices
articles.summary: The Irish Data Protection Commission has opened a formal investigation into whether a major international law firm violated GDPR by using client documents to fine-tune an internal AI model without adequate data processing agreements.

articles.title: Pro Bono Programs Tap AI to Expand Access to Immigration Advice
articles.summary: Several large law firms are deploying AI-assisted intake tools for pro bono immigration cases, allowing volunteer attorneys to handle three times the caseload and meaningfully narrowing the access to justice gap for asylum seekers.

articles.title: Bar Associations Push Law Schools to Add Negotiation and Tech Skills
articles.summary: The ABA's task force on future lawyer competencies is recommending that accredited law schools require coursework in technology literacy, project management and alternative fee arrangements as conditions of accreditation renewal.
